scope.summary: Implement image-input garment authoring path in TailorModelAdapter: accepts an image artifact_id (reference photo or sketch) alongside an optional text brief, passes both to a vision-capable LlmClient call, and produces a GarmentSpec from the visual decomposition
acceptance_criteria: AuthorGarmentInput struct gains an optional reference_image_artifact_id: Option<String> field alongside the existing spec field

When reference_image_artifact_id is non-null and spec is None, TailorModelAdapter constructs a CompletionRequest with the image bytes inline and prompts the VLM to output a GarmentSpec JSON or GarmentCodeRC compact payload
The image path passes through the identical sandbox -> fast-validation -> author_garment -> TailorGarmentDraftProposed lifecycle as the text path
When both spec and reference_image_artifact_id are provided, the image is used as a reference for NGL planning only; the explicit spec takes priority for panel vertices
The vision-capable path is guarded by a feature flag (vision-authoring) and gracefully returns a typed error (InvalidSpec with message 'vision-authoring feature not enabled') when disabled
ModelManual entry for author_garment is updated to describe the optional image input parameter and its precedence rules
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::model_adapter::image_input_path -- --nocapture (image-only input produces a draft garment via mocked VLM response)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::model_adapter::image_feature_flag_off (returns typed error when vision-authoring feature disabled)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::mcp_tools::author_garment_schema_includes_image_field (schemars JsonSchema for AuthorGarmentInput includes reference_image_artifact_id)
scope.risk_if_missed: The image-to-pattern authoring path (Design2GarmentCode, Dress-1-to-3 capture mode) is absent. Operators cannot hand Tailor a reference garment photo or sketch and get a GarmentSpec back. All authoring requires the operator or model to manually describe panel vertices in JSON, which blocks the most commercially valuable workflow: 'match this garment from a photo.'

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
